tickets.title: Build the OpenCode rails-guard plugin MVP (per the opencode-integration design)
tickets.body:
Build the FIRST shippable increment of the OpenCode integration: the in-process rails-guard plugin plus the discovery skill, implementing the unbuilt core of the existing design. This ticket was reconciled (2026-06-27) against substantial prior work on branch `opencode-integration` — DO NOT re-derive the design or rebuild what already exists.

=== AUTHORITATIVE DESIGN (the contract — read first) ===
The full OpenCode integration is already designed on branch `opencode-integration` (NOT yet merged to main):
- docs/opencode-integration-plan.md — the 6-phase (A-F) delivery plan and the in-process hook contract. THIS is the design contract; do not contradict it.
- docs/opencode.md — the adoption guide.
Read both via `git show opencode-integration:docs/opencode-integration-plan.md` and `git show opencode-integration:docs/opencode.md`. Treat their primitive mapping (§3), directory structure (§4.1), hook contract (§4.2), and phased plan (§7) as the spec.

=== WHAT ALREADY EXISTS — DO NOT REBUILD (reconciliation) ===
1. Phase F CI backstop is IMPLEMENTED on `opencode-integration`: docs/ci/rails-guard.yml (hardened: codeowners attestation, signed runner pool, trust-root freeze, monotonic config invariants), scripts/rails-guard-ci.mjs (+295), and ~1800 lines of tests (scripts/test/rails-guard-bootstrap-ci.test.mjs, scripts/test/rails-guard-ci.test.mjs). The unbypassable second enforcement layer therefore ALREADY EXISTS. Do not re-author it. The ONLY deferred CI item relevant here (plan §6.2) is protecting `.opencode/plugin/` and `.opencode/opencode.json` paths — note it as Phase F follow-up; do not implement it in this ticket.
2. @adlc/core (v1.1.0) is published and is the single source of truth for rails logic (plan §6.6). The plugin MUST delegate to it, NOT inline-port the adlc-pi helpers (the duplicated legacy CC/codex hooks are explicitly flagged as tech debt to remove).

=== RESOLVED OpenCode plugin API (P1 research + plan, pin in ADR 0004) ===
- Package: @opencode-ai/plugin v1.17.11 (peerDependency). Backend is Bun/JS in-process (plan §2).
- Discovery/load: the SHIPPED source lives in this repo at plugins/adlc-opencode/ (mirroring plugins/adlc-codex, plugins/adlc-pi); /adlc-init copies/symlinks it into the user's .opencode/plugin/adlc-opencode/ and registers it in .opencode/opencode.json (plan §4.1, Phase A). OpenCode loads the pre-bundled dist/index.js.
- Edit-interception hook: "tool.execute.before", async (input, output) => {...}. VERIFY-FIRST and pin in ADR 0004: (i) whether the edited path is input.args.filePath or output.args.filePath (docs vs gist conflict) — capture a real edit AND write payload; (ii) the full set of file-mutating tool names in v1.17.11 (edit/write/patch/multiedit/apply_patch).
- CRITICAL ENFORCEMENT-CAPABILITY GATE (plan §Phase D — resolves the earlier 'does throw abort the write?' question): the in-session hook MUST NOT be labelled/treated as enforcing unless the installed OpenCode SDK supports `onFailure: deny` AND a runtime capability probe confirms it. If unsupported, the hook is ADVISORY only and preflight FAILS CLOSED unless `allowAdvisoryHooks: true` is explicitly configured. The unbypassable layer remains the CI gate (already built).
- Workspace root: PIN which plugin context field (project/directory/worktree) is the repo root for locating .adlc/.

=== RAIL CONTRACT — delegate to @adlc/core, preserve sibling semantics ===
The thin adapter (rails-checker.ts) maps OpenCode hook args to @adlc/core and must preserve the contract both sibling hooks implement (plugins/adlc-codex/hooks/adlc-rails-guard.mjs, plugins/adlc-pi/index.ts):
1. Active ticket via process.env.ADLC_TICKET OR .adlc/current-ticket.json; conflict => fail closed.
2. Enforce only when process.env.ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT === '1'; else no-op.
3. Rails = the SINGLE active ticket's rails PLUS implicit trust-root rails '.adlc/tickets.json' and '.adlc/current-ticket.json'. NOT a union across tickets.
4. No-op fail-safe when .adlc/tickets.json absent, enforcement off, no active ticket, or empty declared rails (trust-root still frozen).
5. On a rail match, deny via the SDK's onFailure:deny contract (per the capability gate above), naming the rail + ticket.

=== DELIVERABLES (this MVP increment only) ===
1. plugins/adlc-opencode/ — Node package mirroring the sibling integrations: package.json (@adlc/opencode-package, type module, peerDependency @opencode-ai/plugin >=1.17.11, dependency @adlc/core), index.ts (the Plugin named export wiring tool.execute.before), rails-checker.ts (thin adapter importing @adlc/core rails logic — NO inlined re-implementation), a pre-bundled dist/index.js, and skill/adlc.md (the phase-routing discovery skill, copied/adapted from the sibling skill sentinels IF OpenCode's skill format matches; verify first).
2. scripts/opencode-install-smoke.mjs — mirrors scripts/codex-install-smoke.mjs / scripts/claude-code-plugin-smoke.mjs: validate the plugin package shape, the dist bundle, the hook wiring, skill registration, and that the deny path delegates to @adlc/core. Must not require the opencode binary and must not mutate the user environment.
3. docs/integrations/opencode.md — replace the 'no integration exists yet' stub with a real landing doc that LINKS the authoritative plan (docs/opencode-integration-plan.md) and docs/opencode.md, documents this MVP increment + the two-layer enforcement (advisory in-session hook gated on onFailure:deny + the already-built mandatory CI gate), Install (local verification via the smoke test), the Formal ADLC Coverage table, and Gaps (the unbuilt Phases A/B/C/E below; the deferred .opencode/plugin path protection).
4. docs/adr/0004-adlc-opencode-integration.md — formal ADR pointing to the plan as the detailed design, pinning the resolved API facts (i)-(ii), the onFailure:deny capability gate, and a Threat Model section (the in-session bypass surface; CI gate as mitigation).

OUT OF SCOPE (now authored as follow-on tickets T2 Phase A / T3 Phase B / T4 Phase C / T5 Phase E — note them in the ADR Gaps, do not build here): the full slash-command suite (/adlc-ticket../adlc-maintain), the keyless two-phase LLM bridge, the advisory session hooks, the 5-lens prosecutor subagents + verifier, and the .opencode/plugin path-protection CI follow-up.

=== ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA ===
- AC1: `node scripts/opencode-install-smoke.mjs .` exits 0 and reports the plugin package, dist bundle, hook wiring, and skill registration valid. VERIFY: run it; assert exit 0.
- AC2: The deny path delegates to @adlc/core (single source of truth) and does NOT re-implement glob/rail matching inline. VERIFY: smoke test asserts rails-checker.ts imports @adlc/core; a grep confirms no inlined globMatch/canonicalizePath copy in the plugin.
- AC3 (enforcement proof, runs by default, no opencode binary): a unit test at plugins/adlc-opencode/test/*.test.mjs invokes the REAL exported tool.execute.before handler with a CAPTURED real payload, under `node --test`, covering and passing: (a) no-op when .adlc/tickets.json absent; (b) no-op when ADLC_P4_ENFORCEMENT != '1'; (c) no-op when no active ticket; (d) DENY an edit AND a write to the active ticket's rail; (e) DENY an edit to .adlc/tickets.json (trust-root); (f) a DIFFERENT ticket's rail does NOT block; (g) conflicting ADLC_TICKET vs current-ticket.json fails closed; (h) when the SDK capability probe reports no onFailure:deny support, the hook is advisory and preflight fails closed unless allowAdvisoryHooks is set. VERIFY: test exercises each via the real handler and exits 0.
- AC4: docs/integrations/opencode.md no longer contains 'no integration exists yet', links docs/opencode-integration-plan.md, and has Install + Formal ADLC Coverage table + Gaps. VERIFY: grep for absence of the banner, presence of the plan link, and the three headings.
- AC5: docs/adr/0004 exists, references the plan, pins the API facts + onFailure:deny gate, and has a '## Threat Model' section. VERIFY: file exists; grep for @opencode-ai/plugin + tool.execute.before + 'onFailure' + '## Threat Model'.
- AC6: No sibling integration regresses. VERIFY: `node scripts/codex-install-smoke.mjs .` and `node scripts/claude-code-plugin-smoke.mjs .` both exit 0.
- AC7: The existing CI backstop is acknowledged, not duplicated. VERIFY: docs/integrations/opencode.md states the unbypassable CI layer is docs/ci/rails-guard.yml (already implemented on opencode-integration) and this ticket adds no competing CI workflow; grep confirms no new .github/workflows or docs/ci file is created by this ticket.

Suppressions are denied. PREREQUISITE: this ticket's build depends on the opencode-integration design + @adlc/core + CI backstop being available on the working base (merge/rebase opencode-integration to main first, or build atop it). If that base is absent, STOP and escalate rather than re-deriving the design.

edges.contract: plugins/adlc-opencode/ shared package (sequential single-session build, plan §6.7)

tickets.title: OpenCode integration Phase A: gate dependency mappings + slash command suite
tickets.body: Implements plan Phase A (docs/opencode-integration-plan.md §7 on branch opencode-integration). Builds on the T1 MVP plugin package. Configure package mappings so first use never ENOENTs: the `adlc` dispatcher, `adlc-runner` (pending merge), and the 19 gate bins (behavior-diff, coldstart, consensus-fix, flail-detector, gate-fuzzing, gate-manifest, hollow-test, lesson-foundry, merge-forecast, model-ratchet, model-router, parallax, preflight, premortem, rails-guard, rejection-mining, review-calibration, skill-rot, spec-lint). Implement the slash-command surface scaffolded into .opencode/command/ by /adlc-init: /adlc-init (scaffolds .adlc/config.json and copies/symlinks command/agent/skill dirs into .opencode/ from the package source), /adlc-ticket (P0), /adlc-approve-spec (P1 G1), /adlc-spec (P1), /adlc-decompose (P2). Acceptance: a smoke test asserts each command file is present and /adlc-init scaffolds the .opencode/ layout per plan §4.1. Read the plan as the contract; do not re-derive it.

edges.contract: plugins/adlc-opencode/ shared package (sequential single-session build, plan §6.7)

tickets.title: OpenCode integration Phase B: keyless two-phase LLM-gate bridge
tickets.body: Implements plan Phase B (§4.3, §7). Builds on the T1 MVP package. Write the plugin SDK hook implementing the two-phase stdio JSON cascade: extract prompts via --prompt-session, falling back to --prompt-only with stdin replay, and route them keylessly to isolated model sub-contexts, handling concurrent multi-round and multi-sample flows. This is the keyless path so model-backed gates need no API key inside OpenCode. Depends on the proposed SDK extension interfaces noted in plan §6.4; if absent, degrade to fresh-context isolation on the active session model and document the limitation. Acceptance: a unit test drives the cascade with a stubbed SDK and asserts prompt extraction + isolated dispatch; required for T5 (prosecutor lenses run in isolated contexts).

edges.contract: plugins/adlc-opencode/ shared package (sequential single-session build, plan §6.7)

tickets.title: OpenCode integration Phase C: advisory session hooks (preflight + manifest audit)
tickets.body: Implements plan Phase C (§7). Builds on the T1 MVP package. Implement the advisory, fail-safe session hooks: session.created (checks runtime/git readiness and environment determinism, warns if not ready for fan-out) and session.ended (runs `gate-manifest verify` and warns only if the evidence chain is broken). Both must no-op cleanly when the repo is not ADLC-initialized. Acceptance: a unit test asserts each hook no-ops without .adlc/ and emits the expected advisory signal when the chain is broken. Read the plan as the contract.

edges.contract: plugins/adlc-opencode/ shared package (sequential single-session build, plan §6.7)

tickets.title: OpenCode integration Phase E: prosecutor lenses, G4 build gate, distill
tickets.body: Implements plan Phase E (§7). Builds on the T1 MVP package and the T3 keyless bridge (the lenses run in isolated model contexts). Configure the G4 build-gate command (/adlc-verify-build) and the runner assertion (adlc-runner run p4) running build/lint/test before prosecution; the fanned-out prosecution subagents (5 lenses: correctness, security, contract, diff, tests) plus the verifier/reproducer agent and the loop orchestration; and /adlc-distill for lesson mining with an optional local --simplify pass (plan §6.2 documents the Simplify deviation and its rail-freeze invariant). Acceptance: smoke test asserts the agent/ lens files and the /adlc-verify-build + /adlc-distill commands are present and wired; a unit test covers the prosecution loop orchestration against stubbed lens outputs. Read the plan as the contract.
